display_name.he: איך כותבים את הסקיל הראשון שלכם

display_description.he: קורס בן 6 פרקים למפתחים: איך לכתוב סקיל סוכן של Claude מאפס, לשחרר אותו לקטלוג skills-il, ולעבור את 10 בדיקות לפני פרסום.
display_description.en: A 6-chapter course for developers on writing a Claude agent skill from scratch, shipping it to the skills-il catalog, and passing the 10 pre-publish checks.

who_is_this_for.he: מפתחים שיודעים git, Markdown, ו־JSON, ורוצים לכתוב סקיל סוכן של Claude לקטלוג skills-il. אין צורך בניסיון קודם עם Claude Skills.
who_is_this_for.en: Developers who know git, Markdown, and JSON, and want to write a Claude agent skill for the skills-il catalog. No prior Claude Skills experience required.

he: להבין מה סקיל באמת הוא ל־LLM ברגע הניתוב, ומתי לבחור בסקיל לעומת MCP / system prompt / CLI
לכתוב SKILL.md עם תיאור שמופעל נכון על ידי ה־LLM (דפוסי Use when / Do NOT use for)
להבדיל בין metadata.json לבין YAML frontmatter ולהבין למה skills-il משתמש בקובץ נפרד
להחליט בין references/ ל־scripts/ לכל תוכן תומך בסקיל
להעביר סקיל מקצה לקצה דרך ה־pipeline: scaffold, push, sync, ציון אמון, פרסום
להריץ רשימת תיוג 10 דקות לפני שחרור ולעבור 10 הסיבות הנפוצות לדחייה
en: Understand what a skill really is to the LLM at routing time, and when to pick a skill vs MCP / system prompt / CLI
Write a SKILL.md with a description the LLM routes correctly (Use when / Do NOT use for patterns)
Distinguish metadata.json from YAML frontmatter and understand why skills-il uses a separate file
Decide between references/ and scripts/ for any supporting content
Ship a skill end-to-end through the pipeline: scaffold, push, sync, trust score, publication
Run the 10-minute pre-publish checklist and pass the 10 most common rejection reasons

images.alt_he: סקיל לעומת MCP לעומת system prompt לעומת CLI
images.alt_en: Skill vs MCP vs system prompt vs CLI
images.prompt: Editorial illustration: a 2x2 grid of four quadrants, each labeled with a tool type and a single tiny icon. Top-left 'Skill' (document with frontmatter lines), top-right 'MCP' (server with network arrows), bottom-left 'System prompt' (chat-bubble icon), bottom-right 'CLI' (terminal cursor). Each quadrant is bordered in navy with a single Israeli-blue accent stripe at the top. Warm beige paper background, flat 2D editorial style, no decoration.

Editorial illustration: a stylized SKILL.md document in the center, with three labeled callouts pointing to its sections. Top callout: 'frontmatter (3 fields: name, description, license)'. Middle callout: 'body (scope, rules, examples)'. Bottom callout: 'optional references/ + scripts/'. Document drawn as a flat rectangle in navy outline. Callout lines and labels in Israeli-blue accent. Warm beige paper background.

Editorial illustration: a flat 2D representation of a JSON file with 8 visible key-value lines labeled with field names: name, version, display_name (he+en), display_description, audience, level, tags (he+en), supported_agents. Use clean sans-serif and tasteful indentation. Single Israeli-blue accent on the display_description line (the most important field). Warm beige paper background, navy text, no syntax-highlighting colors beyond the one accent.

Editorial illustration: a vertical decision tree with one root question 'What kind of content is it?'. Two branches descending. Left branch labeled 'Deterministic compute or network call?' leading to a box labeled 'scripts/'. Right branch labeled 'Static lookup or template?' leading to a box labeled 'references/'. Arrows in navy with single Israeli-blue accent. Warm beige paper background, minimal.

images.alt_he: תהליך הפרסום: מסקפולד עד ציון אמון
images.alt_en: Publication flow: scaffold to trust score
images.prompt: Editorial illustration: a horizontal timeline with 5 nodes connected by navy arrows, labeled in order: 'Scaffold', 'Validate locally', 'Push to category repo', 'Sync to catalog', 'Trust score + publish'. Each node is a small circle. The 'Trust score + publish' node is highlighted with Israeli-blue. Warm beige paper background, flat 2D editorial style, clean sans-serif.

Editorial illustration: a vertical checklist with 10 small navy-outline checkboxes followed by short labels. The first 3 items are checked (Israeli-blue checkmark). Items top to bottom: 'em-dash grep', 'slug matches folder', 'description aloud', 'Hebrew naturalness', 'numbers verified', 'math redone', 'files exist', 'agent icons render', 'version bumped', 'validator run'. Warm beige paper background, navy text, single Israeli-blue accent.
